The monk was sentenced to seven years in prison, but was released because of mental illnesses ([[:en:persecution complex|persecution complex]] and [[:en:schizophrenia|schizophrenia]]) on 29 September 1955; he died of [[:en:tuberculosis|tuberculosis]] in March 1956.<ref><nowiki>{{cite book|author=Albert Borowitz|title=Terrorism for self-glorification: the Herostratos syndrome|url=</nowiki>https://archive.org/details/terrorismforself0000boro%7Curl-access=registration%7Caccess-date=1 July 2011|year=2005|publisher=Kent State University Press|isbn=978-0-87338-818-4|pages=[https://archive.org/details/terrorismforself0000boro/page/49 49]–62]] See: [[:en:Herostratos syndrome|Herostratos syndrome]]</ref> During the fire, the original statue of Ashikaga Yoshimitsu was lost to the flames (now restored). A fictionalized version of these events is at the center of [[:en:Yukio Mishima|Yukio Mishima]]'s 1956 book ''[[:en:The Temple of the Golden Pavilion|The Temple of the Golden Pavilion]]'',<ref name="orientalarch"/> and another in the ballet ''[[:en:RAkU (ballet)| RAkU]]''.

[[Category:July 1950]] [[Category:Japanese incidents in 1950]] [[Category:Applegate crime]] [[Category:History of Kita-ku, Kyoto]] [[Category:Arson in Japan]] [[Category:Kyoto in Postwar Shōwa]] [[Category:Kinkaku-ji]] <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> [[en:Kinkaku-ji arson]] [[vi:Phóng hỏa ở chùa Kinkaku]] [[zh:金閣寺放火事件]] [[Category:translated pages with valid en interwikis]] {{wikidata link|Q11647990}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Kinkaku-ji arson]][[ja:金閣寺放火事件]][[vi:Phóng hỏa ở chùa Kinkaku]][[zh:金閣寺放火事件]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Kinkaku-ji arson]][[ja:金閣寺放火事件]][[vi:Phóng hỏa ở chùa Kinkaku]][[zh:金閣寺放火事件]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11647990]] Wikidata name: Kinkaku-ji arson incident [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:32, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11647990]] Wikidata name: Kinkaku-ji arson incident [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:12, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} On 2 July 1950, at 2:30 am, the pavilion was burned down<ref name="whe1">{{cite web |last1=Cartwright |first1=Mark |title=Kinkakuji |url=https://www.worldhistory.org/Kinkakuji/ |website=World History Encyclopedia |publisher=UNESCO |access-date=23 March 2021]]</ref> by a 22-year-old novice monk, Hayashi Yoken ({{ill|Kinkaku-ji arson incident|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinkaku-ji arson incident|qq=}} ), who then attempted suicide on the Daimon-ji hill behind the building. He survived, and was subsequently taken into custody. The monk was sentenced to seven years in prison, but was released because of mental illnesses ([[:en:persecution complex|persecution complex]] and [[:en:schizophrenia|schizophrenia]]) on 29 September 1955; he died of [[:en:tuberculosis|tuberculosis]] in March 1956.<ref><nowiki>{{cite book|author=Albert Borowitz|title=Terrorism for self-glorification: the Herostratos syndrome|url=</nowiki>https://archive.org/details/terrorismforself0000boro%7Curl-access=registration%7Caccess-date=1 July 2011|year=2005|publisher=Kent State University Press|isbn=978-0-87338-818-4|pages=[https://archive.org/details/terrorismforself0000boro/page/49 49]–62]] See: [[:en:Herostratos syndrome|Herostratos syndrome]]</ref> During the fire, the original statue of Ashikaga Yoshimitsu was lost to the flames (now restored). A fictionalized version of these events is at the center of [[:en:Yukio Mishima|Yukio Mishima]]'s 1956 book ''[[:en:The Temple of the Golden Pavilion|The Temple of the Golden Pavilion]]'',<ref name="orientalarch"/> and another in the ballet ''[[:en:RAkU (ballet)| RAkU]]''.
{{translated page|ja|金閣寺放火事件|version=104521219|comment=Imported full ja history}}
{{draft categories||qq=draft}}

He survived, and was subsequently taken into custody. The monk was sentenced to seven years in prison, but was released because of mental illnesses ([[:en:persecution complex|persecution complex]] and [[:en:schizophrenia|schizophrenia]]) on 29 September 1955; he died of [[:en:tuberculosis|tuberculosis]] in March 1956.<ref><nowiki>{{cite book|author=Albert Borowitz|title=Terrorism for self-glorification: the Herostratos syndrome|url=</nowiki>https://archive.org/details/terrorismforself0000boro%7Curl-access=registration%7Caccess-date=1 July 2011|year=2005|publisher=Kent State University Press|isbn=978-0-87338-818-4|pages=[https://archive.org/details/terrorismforself0000boro/page/49 49]–62]] See: [[:en:Herostratos syndrome|Herostratos syndrome]]</ref> During the fire, the original statue of Ashikaga Yoshimitsu was lost to the flames (now restored). A fictionalized version of these events is at the center of [[:en:Yukio Mishima|Yukio Mishima]]'s 1956 book ''[[:en:The Temple of the Golden Pavilion|The Temple of the Golden Pavilion]]'',<ref name="orientalarch"/> and another in the ballet ''[[:en:RAkU (ballet)| RAkU]]''.

revision.comment: v25: Convert interwiki links [[:en:...]] to {{ill}} templates
revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Kinkaku-ji arson]][[ja:金閣寺放火事件]][[vi:Phóng hỏa ở chùa Kinkaku]][[zh:金閣寺放火事件]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Kinkaku-ji arson]][[ja:金閣寺放火事件]][[vi:Phóng hỏa ở chùa Kinkaku]][[zh:金閣寺放火事件]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Kinkaku-ji arson]][[ja:金閣寺放火事件]][[vi:Phóng hỏa ở chùa Kinkaku]][[zh:金閣寺放火事件]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11647990]] Wikidata name: Kinkaku-ji arson incident [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:32, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11647990]] Wikidata name: Kinkaku-ji arson incident [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:12, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} On 2 July 1950, at 2:30 am, the pavilion was burned down<ref name="whe1">{{cite web |last1=Cartwright |first1=Mark |title=Kinkakuji |url=https://www.worldhistory.org/Kinkakuji/ |website=World History Encyclopedia |publisher=UNESCO |access-date=23 March 2021]]</ref> by a 22-year-old novice monk, Hayashi Yoken ({{ill|Kinkaku-ji arson incident|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinkaku-ji arson incident|qq=}} ), who then attempted suicide on the Daimon-ji hill behind the building. He survived, and was subsequently taken into custody. The monk was sentenced to seven years in prison, but was released because of mental illnesses ([[:en:persecution complex|persecution complex]] and {{ill|schizophrenia|en|schizophrenia|WD=Q41112|lt=schizophrenia}}) on 29 September 1955; he died of {{ill|tuberculosis|en|tuberculosis|WD=Q12204|lt=tuberculosis}} in March 1956.<ref><nowiki>{{cite book|author=Albert Borowitz|title=Terrorism for self-glorification: the Herostratos syndrome|url=</nowiki>https://archive.org/details/terrorismforself0000boro%7Curl-access=registration%7Caccess-date=1 July 2011|year=2005|publisher=Kent State University Press|isbn=978-0-87338-818-4|pages=[https://archive.org/details/terrorismforself0000boro/page/49 49]–62]] See: [[:en:Herostratos syndrome|Herostratos syndrome]]</ref> During the fire, the original statue of Ashikaga Yoshimitsu was lost to the flames (now restored). A fictionalized version of these events is at the center of {{ill|Yukio Mishima|en|Yukio Mishima|WD=Q134456|lt=Yukio Mishima}}'s 1956 book ''{{ill|The Temple of the Golden Pavilion|en|The Temple of the Golden Pavilion|WD=Q2518946|lt=The Temple of the Golden Pavilion}}'',<ref name="orientalarch"/> and another in the ballet ''{{ill|RAkU (ballet)|en|RAkU (ballet)|WD=Q21161089|lt= RAkU}}''.
{{translated page|ja|金閣寺放火事件|version=104521219|comment=Imported full ja history}}
{{draft categories||qq=draft}}

He survived, and was subsequently taken into custody. The monk was sentenced to seven years in prison, but was released because of mental illnesses ([[:en:persecution complex|persecution complex]] and {{ill|schizophrenia|en|schizophrenia|WD=Q41112|lt=schizophrenia}}) on 29 September 1955; he died of {{ill|tuberculosis|en|tuberculosis|WD=Q12204|lt=tuberculosis}} in March 1956.<ref><nowiki>{{cite book|author=Albert Borowitz|title=Terrorism for self-glorification: the Herostratos syndrome|url=</nowiki>https://archive.org/details/terrorismforself0000boro%7Curl-access=registration%7Caccess-date=1 July 2011|year=2005|publisher=Kent State University Press|isbn=978-0-87338-818-4|pages=[https://archive.org/details/terrorismforself0000boro/page/49 49]–62]] See: [[:en:Herostratos syndrome|Herostratos syndrome]]</ref> During the fire, the original statue of Ashikaga Yoshimitsu was lost to the flames (now restored). A fictionalized version of these events is at the center of {{ill|Yukio Mishima|en|Yukio Mishima|WD=Q134456|lt=Yukio Mishima}}'s 1956 book ''{{ill|The Temple of the Golden Pavilion|en|The Temple of the Golden Pavilion|WD=Q2518946|lt=The Temple of the Golden Pavilion}}'',<ref name="orientalarch"/> and another in the ballet ''{{ill|RAkU (ballet)|en|RAkU (ballet)|WD=Q21161089|lt= RAkU}}''.

On 2 July 1950, at 2:30 am, the pavilion was burned down<ref name="whe1">{{cite web |last1=Cartwright |first1=Mark |title=Kinkakuji |url=https://www.worldhistory.org/Kinkakuji/ |website=World History Encyclopedia |publisher=UNESCO |access-date=23 March 2021]]</ref> by a 22-year-old novice monk, Hayashi Yoken ({{ill|Kinkaku-ji arson incident|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinkaku-ji arson incident|qq=}} ), who then attempted suicide on the Daimon-ji hill behind the building. He survived, and was subsequently taken into custody. The monk was sentenced to seven years in prison, but was released because of mental illnesses ([[:en:persecution complex|persecution complex]] and {{ill|schizophrenia|en|schizophrenia|WD=Q41112|lt=schizophrenia}}) on 29 September 1955; he died of {{ill|tuberculosis|en|tuberculosis|WD=Q12204|lt=tuberculosis}} in March 1956.<ref><nowiki>{{cite book|author=Albert Borowitz|title=Terrorism for self-glorification: the Herostratos syndrome|url=</nowiki>https://archive.org/details/terrorismforself0000boro%7Curl-access=registration%7Caccess-date=1 July 2011|year=2005|publisher=Kent State University Press|isbn=978-0-87338-818-4|pages=[https://archive.org/details/terrorismforself0000boro/page/49 49]–62]] See: [[:en:Herostratos syndrome|Herostratos syndrome]]</ref> During the fire, the original statue of Ashikaga Yoshimitsu was lost to the flames (now restored). A fictionalized version of these events is at the center of {{ill|Yukio Mishima|en|Yukio Mishima|WD=Q134456|lt=Yukio Mishima}}'s 1956 book ''{{ill|The Temple of the Golden Pavilion|en|The Temple of the Golden Pavilion|WD=Q2518946|lt=The Temple of the Golden Pavilion}}'',<ref name="orientalarch"/> and another in the ballet ''{{ill|RAkU (ballet)|en|RAkU (ballet)|WD=Q21161089|lt= RAkU}}''.

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Kinkaku-ji arson]][[ja:金閣寺放火事件]][[vi:Phóng hỏa ở chùa Kinkaku]][[zh:金閣寺放火事件]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Kinkaku-ji arson]][[ja:金閣寺放火事件]][[vi:Phóng hỏa ở chùa Kinkaku]][[zh:金閣寺放火事件]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Kinkaku-ji arson]][[ja:金閣寺放火事件]][[vi:Phóng hỏa ở chùa Kinkaku]][[zh:金閣寺放火事件]]
On 2 July 1950, at 2:30 am, the pavilion was burned down<ref name="whe1">{{cite web |last1=Cartwright |first1=Mark |title=Kinkakuji |url=https://www.worldhistory.org/Kinkakuji/ |website=World History Encyclopedia |publisher=UNESCO |access-date=23 March 2021]]</ref> by a 22-year-old novice monk, Hayashi Yoken ({{ill|Kinkaku-ji arson incident|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinkaku-ji arson incident|qq=}} ), who then attempted suicide on the Daimon-ji hill behind the building. He survived, and was subsequently taken into custody. The monk was sentenced to seven years in prison, but was released because of mental illnesses ([[:en:persecution complex|persecution complex]] and {{ill|schizophrenia|en|schizophrenia|WD=Q41112|lt=schizophrenia|1=Schizophrenia}}) on 29 September 1955; he died of {{ill|tuberculosis|en|tuberculosis|WD=Q12204|lt=tuberculosis|1=Tuberculosis}} in March 1956.<ref><nowiki>{{cite book|author=Albert Borowitz|title=Terrorism for self-glorification: the Herostratos syndrome|url=</nowiki>https://archive.org/details/terrorismforself0000boro%7Curl-access=registration%7Caccess-date=1 July 2011|year=2005|publisher=Kent State University Press|isbn=978-0-87338-818-4|pages=[https://archive.org/details/terrorismforself0000boro/page/49 49]–62]] See: [[:en:Herostratos syndrome|Herostratos syndrome]]</ref> During the fire, the original statue of Ashikaga Yoshimitsu was lost to the flames (now restored). A fictionalized version of these events is at the center of {{ill|Yukio Mishima|en|Yukio Mishima|WD=Q134456|lt=Yukio Mishima|1=Yukio Mishima}}'s 1956 book ''{{ill|The Temple of the Golden Pavilion|en|The Temple of the Golden Pavilion|WD=Q2518946|lt=The Temple of the Golden Pavilion|1=The Temple of the Golden Pavilion}}'',<ref name="orientalarch"/> and another in the ballet ''{{ill|RAkU (ballet)|en|RAkU (ballet)|WD=Q21161089|lt= RAkU|1=RAkU (ballet)}}''.
{{translated page|ja|金閣寺放火事件|version=104521219|comment=Imported full ja history}}
{{draft categories||qq=draft}}
